name: Taj Mahal
description: One of the Seven Wonders of the World, a symbol of eternal love built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

name: Qutub Minar
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this 73-metre tall minaret is the tallest brick minaret in the world.

name: India Gate
description: An iconic war memorial built in the memory of Indian soldiers who died in World War I.

name: Red Fort
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this massive red sandstone fort served as the main residence of the Mughal emperors.

name: Lotus Temple
description: A magnificent lotus-shaped temple that welcomes people of all religions for prayer and meditation.

name: Jama Masjid
description: One of the largest mosques in India, capable of holding 25,000 worshippers at a time.

name: Humayun Tomb
description: A magnificent mid-16th century Mughal mausoleum in Delhi, built for the Mughal Emperor Humayun.

name: Akshardham Temple
description: A stunning Hindu temple complex and cultural campus on the banks of the Yamuna River.

name: Lal Qila
description: Also known as Red Fort, a historic fort that served as the main residence of Mughal emperors for nearly 200 years.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: A historic street in Old Delhi famous for its food, street markets, and vibrant culture.

name: Amber Fort
description: A stunning hilltop fort overlooking Maota Lake, a blend of Hindu and Mughal architecture.

name: City Palace Jaipur
description: A magnificent palace complex in Jaipur, home to the royal family of Jaipur.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: The Palace of Winds, a stunning five-story palace with 953 small windows (jharokhas) decorated with intricate latticework.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
description: One of the most magnificent and well-preserved forts in India, located in the blue city of Jodhpur.

name: Jaisalmer Fort
description: A desert fort that rises from the sands of the Thar Desert, also known as the Golden Fort.

name: Lake Palace Udaipur
description: A stunning palace hotel built in the middle of Lake Pichola in Udaipur, the City of Lakes.

name: Chittorgarh Fort
description: A magnificent 7th-century fort complex in Rajasthan, considered the pride of Rajputs.

name: Ranthambore Tiger Reserve
description: One of the finest wildlife reserves in India, known for Bengal tigers.

name: Pushkar Lake
description: A sacred city built around a lake, famous for the Brahma Temple and annual Pushkar Camel Fair.

name: Jantar Mantar
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, an astronomical instrument collection built in the 18th century.

name: Hampi
description: An ancient city that was once the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Mysore Palace
description: A magnificent palace in Mysore, one of the largest palaces in India, illuminated by 100,000 bulbs.

name: Brihadeeswarar Temple
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the greatest temples of India, dedicated to Lord Shiva.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: A magnificent temple dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi, known for its stunning colorful gopurams.

name: Shore Temple Mahabalipuram
description: A group of ancient temples built by the Pallava kings, overlooking the Bay of Bengal.

name: Coorg
description: A hill station in Karnataka known for its coffee plantations, misty valleys, and waterfalls.

name: Ooty
description: A picturesque hill station in Tamil Nadu, known as the Queen of Hill Stations.

name: Munnar
description: A breathtakingly beautiful hill station in Kerala, famous for its lush tea plantations.

name: Kodaikanal
description: A charming hill station in Tamil Nadu, the Princess of Hill Stations, surrounded by forests.

name: Wayanad
description: A pristine wildlife sanctuary in Kerala with dense forests, wildlife, and tribal communities.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
description: Known as the Venice of the East, famous for its serene backwater network and houseboat experiences.

name: Kovalam Beach
description: A beautiful beach town in Kerala with stunning shores and a vibrant fishing community.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A wildlife sanctuary centered around Periyar Lake, home to elephants and tigers.

name: Varkala Beach
description: A cliff-side beach town famous for its laterite cliffs, natural springs, and peaceful atmosphere.

name: Thekkady
description: A beautiful wildlife sanctuary in Kerala, famous for elephants, spice plantations, and boating.

name: Fort Kochi
description: A historic port city with colonial architecture, Chinese fishing nets, and vibrant culture.

name: Athirapally Falls
description: One of India's most spectacular waterfalls, surrounded by dense forests and wildlife.

name: Kalpetta
description: A tranquil hill town in Wayanad, surrounded by misty mountains and coffee plantations.

name: Bekal Fort
description: A beautiful beach fort in Kerala, built by Portuguese traders in the 16th century.

name: Marari Beach
description: A pristine beach in Kerala, known for its untouched beauty and coconut palms.

name: Calangute Beach
description: India's most popular tourist beach, known for its vibrant atmosphere and water sports.

name: Baga Beach
description: A lively beach in Goa, famous for its nightlife, restaurants, and water sports.

name: Anjuna Beach
description: A bohemian beach in North Goa, famous for its flea markets and hippie culture.

name: Palolem Beach
description: One of the most beautiful beaches in Goa, a crescent-shaped bay with pristine sands.

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, the oldest church in India with the mortal remains of St. Francis Xavier.

name: Dudhsagar Falls
description: One of Goa's most spectacular waterfalls, surrounded by spice plantations.

name: Chapora Fort
description: A scenic clifftop fort in North Goa with stunning views of the Arabian Sea.

name: Colva Beach
description: A peaceful beach in South Goa with a relaxed atmosphere and scenic views.

name: Candolim Beach
description: A clean, less-crowded beach in North Goa with beautiful white sands.

name: Vagator Beach
description: A dramatic beach with towering red and black volcanic cliffs in North Goa.

name: Shimla
description: The summer capital of British India, a charming hill station in Himachal Pradesh.

name: Manali
description: A popular hill station in the Kullu Valley, gateway to the high Himalayas.

name: Dharamsala
description: Home of the Dalai Lama and the Tibetan government in exile, a spiritual and cultural hub.

name: Rohtang Pass
description: A high mountain pass in the Himalayas, offering stunning views of snowcapped peaks.

name: Solang Valley
description: A beautiful valley with adventure activities like skiing, zorbing, and paragliding.

name: Kasol
description: A picturesque village on the banks of the Parvati River, a paradise for trekkers.

name: Spiti Valley
description: A remote and starkly beautiful valley in the Himalayas, one of the coldest places in India.

name: Kullu
description: A scenic valley town in Himachal Pradesh, adventure hub of the western Himalayas.

name: Dalhousie
description: A beautiful hill station in Himachal Pradesh, known as the Scotland of India.

name: Khajjiar
description: A small tourist destination often called the Mini Switzerland of India.

name: Rishikesh
description: The Yoga Capital of the World, famous for yoga, meditation, and Ganga Aarti.

name: Haridwar
description: One of the holiest cities in India, on the banks of the Ganges, famous for Ganga Aarti.

name: Mussoorie
description: The Queen of Hills, a scenic hill station in Uttarakhand with toy train and colonial architecture.

name: Nainital
description: A beautiful lake city in the Kumaon Himalayas, surrounded by forests.

name: Jim Corbett National Park
description: The oldest national park in Asia, famous for Bengal tigers and elephant safaris.

name: Auli
description: A ski resort in Uttarakhand with breathtaking views of the Garhwal Himalayas.

name: Valley of Flowers
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, a valley of hundreds of wild flower species in the Himalayas.

name: Kedarnath Temple
description: A sacred Shiva temple in the Garhwal Himalayas, one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites.

name: Badrinath Temple
description: One of the holiest temples in Hinduism, dedicated to Lord Vishnu, in the Garhwal Himalayas.

name: Chopta
description: A beautiful meadow in Uttarakhand, a perfect trek for beginners.

name: Gateway of India
description: A massive arch built in 1924 by the British to commemorate the landing of King George V.

name: Marine Drive Mumbai
description: A 3.6 km long boulevard along the coast of Mumbai, the most beautiful promenade in India.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: A group of cave temples on Elephanta Island near Mumbai, dedicated to Lord Shiva.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site with 30 rock-cut Buddhist caves from the 2nd century BCE.

name: Ellora Caves
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site with 34 cave temples dedicated to Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain traditions.

name: Lonavala
description: A popular hill station near Mumbai, famous for its scenic landscapes and waterfalls.

name: Mahabaleshwar
description: A hill station in the Sahyadri range, known as the Strawberry Capital of India.

name: Aurangabad
description: A city known as the gateway to the Ajanta and Ellora caves.

name: Shirdi
description: A holy town in Maharashtra, home to the Sai Baba temple.

name: Pune
description: The Oxford of the East, a vibrant city with numerous educational institutions and IT companies.

name: Kaziranga National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, home to the Indian one-horned rhinoceros.

name: Cherrapunji
description: The wettest place on Earth, famous for its living root bridges and meghalayan caves.

name: Shillong
description: The capital of Meghalaya, a beautiful hill city with colonial architecture.

name: Tawang Monastery
description: A Buddhist monastery near the China border, the largest monastery in the country.

name: Ziro Valley
description: A remote valley in Arunachal Pradesh, home to the Apatani tribe.

name: Majuli Island
description: The largest river island in the world, known for its Vaishnavite monasteries and culture.

name: Dzukou Valley
description: A high-altitude valley in Nagaland with stunning landscapes and rich biodiversity.

name: Loktak Lake
description: A freshwater lake in Manipur, home to floating islands and a unique ecosystem.

name: Kamakhya Temple
description: A famous Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Kamakhya, a major pilgrimage site in Assam.

name: Haflong
description: A scenic hill station in Assam, surrounded by dense forests and picturesque lakes.

name: Radhanagar Beach Andaman
description: Rated as one of the best beaches in Asia, with crystal clear turquoise waters.

name: Havelock Island
description: The most popular island in the Andaman archipelago, with stunning beaches and marine life.

name: Cellular Jail Andaman
description: A historical prison built during British rule, a National Memorial Monument.

name: Ross Island
description: The former capital of the Andaman Islands, with ruins of British buildings.

name: Neil Island
description: A serene island known for its beautiful beaches and coral reefs.

name: Lakshadweep
description: A group of coral islands in the Arabian Sea, known for crystal clear waters and marine biodiversity.

name: Pondicherry
description: A French colonial city on the southeastern coast of India, known as the 'French Riviera of the East'.

name: Mahabalipuram
description: An ancient coastal town with seven 7th-century rock-cut temples on the beach.

name: Rameshwaram
description: A sacred pilgrimage site where Adam Bridge connecting India to Sri Lanka begins.

name: Kanyakumari
description: The southernmost tip of India where three seas meet - Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean.
